summary: Hostname-gated AES-decrypted code execution backdoor in ts-form-utils
details: On require/load, index.js reads os.hostname() and, when it equals 'devbox' or 'ubuntu-fc-uvm' or contains the substring 'devbox', reads the shipped opaque blob lib/.perf.dat (9168 bytes, high-entropy), AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key (IV taken from the first 16 bytes of the ciphertext), and executes the plaintext via new Function(_r)(). Module names ('crypto', 'fs', 'aes-256-cbc') are split-string-concatenated to evade keyword scanning, and the entire block is wrapped in a silent try/catch so failures produce no output. The package self-describes as a TypeScript form validation utility — there is no legitimate reason for it to perform hostname checks, AES decryption of a hidden dotfile, or dynamic code evaluation. The combination of target-gating on specific developer/CI hostnames, obfuscated module loads, an encrypted opaque payload disguised as 'performance data', and decrypt-then-eval is a textbook backdoor primitive that grants attacker-controlled code execution on any installer whose machine name matches the gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
